title: Reading and Use of English – Part 8
description: You are going to read an article about four people who overcame health challenges through lifestyle changes. For questions 47–56, choose from the people A–D. The people may be chosen more than once.

texts.A:
### Marco, The Yoga Path
'I had chronic insomnia that was ruining my career,' Marco explains. [47]Initially, I was deeply skeptical that stretching in a warm room could solve a clinical sleep issue.[/47] [53]I had to unlearn my competitive nature, which was actually fueling my stress instead of relieving it.[/53] [50]The most helpful aspect was the breathing techniques, which I now use during high-pressure meetings at work.[/50] [55]It took nearly three months before I noticed a consistent improvement in my rest, which tested my patience.[/55] [49]I’m now convinced that mental stillness is just as vital as physical exertion for overall health.[/49] It was a slow transformation, but a permanent one.
texts.B:
### Julia, The Swimming Recovery
After a knee injury, Julia took up swimming. 'I hated the smell of chlorine,' she admits. [51]The biggest obstacle was the logistics of getting to the pool before my long commute every single day.[/51] [48]I was amazed by how the weightlessness in the water relieved pain that expensive physiotherapy hadn't touched.[/48] [56]If my brother hadn't been a competitive swimmer and encouraged me, I would have quit after the first week.[/56] [50]I’ve found that the rhythmic nature of the laps provides a clarity of thought I never found while running.[/50] [52]I hadn't realized how much I’d missed being part of a sporting community until I joined the local club.[/52] It’s been a life-saver.
texts.C:
### Liam, The Plant-Based Shift
Liam changed his diet following a cardiac scare. 'I was a meat-and-potatoes guy,' he says. [47]I was quite concerned that a restricted diet would make social occasions incredibly awkward or dull.[/47] [54]I found that my energy levels spiked almost immediately, which was a sensation I hadn't felt since my teenage years.[/54] [52]I was pleasantly surprised by the variety of flavors I’d been ignoring for forty years in favor of convenience.[/52] [48]The most unexpected benefit was the disappearance of the persistent skin rashes that had plagued me for a decade.[/48] [49]This journey taught me that food is fuel, but it can also be a profound form of self-respect.[/49] I feel younger now than I did ten years ago.
texts.D:
### Nina, The Hiking Cure
Nina struggled with burnout and anxiety. 'The city felt like it was closing in on me,' she recalls. [53]I realized that my recovery depended on disconnecting from the digital world entirely for several hours a week.[/53] [51]The physical difficulty of steep climbs was a distraction that forced my brain to stop overthinking.[/51] [54]I noticed that my mood improved drastically after just one afternoon in the forest, which was a revelation.[/54] [55]I had to be disciplined about not checking my phone, which was harder than the actual walking at first.[/55] [56]I wouldn't have discovered my love for the outdoors if a friend hadn't literally driven me to the trailhead.[/56] Nature has become my primary medicine.

questions.question: mention a fear that the change would negatively impact their social life or be ineffective?
questions.explanation: Liam (C) was concerned about social occasions, and Marco (A) was skeptical about the effectiveness of yoga.

questions.question: describe a positive physical change they hadn't specifically anticipated?
questions.explanation: Liam (C) mentions skin rashes disappearing, and Julia (B) mentions pain relief physiotherapy didn't achieve.

questions.question: express a new philosophical understanding of the role of health in their life?
questions.explanation: Marco (A) mentions the importance of mental stillness, and Liam (C) views food as a form of self-respect.

questions.question: note that a specific technique or habit has been useful in other areas of life?
questions.explanation: Marco (A) uses breathing in meetings, and Julia (B) finds clarity of thought in laps.

questions.question: mention a practical or mental barrier that was difficult to overcome initially?
questions.explanation: Julia (B) mentions pool logistics, and Nina (D) mentions the distraction of overthinking.

questions.question: mention the importance of social connection or a sense of belonging?
questions.explanation: Julia (B) missed being part of a sporting community, and Liam (C) found new flavors (variety).

questions.question: admit that they had to change an ingrained personality trait or habit?
questions.explanation: Marco (A) had to unlearn his competitive nature, and Nina (D) had to disconnect from the digital world.

questions.question: state that some benefits were felt almost instantaneously?
questions.explanation: Liam (C) says energy spiked almost immediately, and Nina (D) saw mood improvement after one afternoon.

questions.question: mention the need for persistence before results became evident?
questions.explanation: Marco (A) waited three months for sleep results, and Nina (D) found staying off her phone hard at first.

questions.question: credit another person for initiating their journey toward better health?
questions.explanation: Julia (B) credits her brother, and Nina (D) credits a friend who drove her to the trailhead.
